mission.title: etcd: Output Inconsistency with json writer
mission.description: Output Inconsistency with json writer . Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current etcd deployment
steps.description:
Verify your etcd version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
helm list -n etcd 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working etcd installation.

steps.title: Review etcd configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant etcd configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get configmap -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=etcd
```
Etcd version:
```
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.crt --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.key --endpoints=[https://127.0.0.1:2379] version
etcdctl version: 3.2.18
API version:

steps.title: Apply the fix for Output Inconsistency with json writer
steps.description:
We also encountered issue #9975 #9535, this pr supports to print memberlist in hex format json and does not break backward compatibility.
It seems to be a simple problem, but it is not so elegant to solve. I prefer to implement Marshal MemberListResponse manually, rather than redefining a redundant
```yaml
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.crt --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.key --endpoints=[https://127.0.0.1:2379] version
etcdctl version: 3.2.18
API version: 3.2
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n etcd -l app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd
kubectl get events -n etcd --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Output Inconsistency with json writer " is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
We also encountered issue #9975 #9535, this pr supports to print memberlist in hex format json and does not break backward compatibility.
It seems to be a simple problem, but it is not so elegant to solve. I prefer to implement Marshal MemberListResponse manually, rather than redefining a redundant structure to implement the member id Marshal method.
codeSnippets:
ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.crt --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.key --endpoints=[https://127.0.0.1:2379] version
etcdctl version: 3.2.18
API version: 3.2

ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl --cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt --cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.crt --key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/peer.key --endpoints=[https://127.0.0.1:2379] member list
3214dd175f565349, started, i-0891498e3531cb084, https://10.97.0.110:2380, https://10.97.0.110:2379
65bf2e476daa66a4, started, i-02ce67ad669ebcab9, https://10.97.3.79:2380, https://10.97.3.79:2379

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with etcd installed or the issue environment reproducible.
